zh.lifeSummary: 當Jean Ier De Picquigny於1247年出生在法國豪-德-福朗斯索姆皮易尼時，他的父親Gérard II de Picquigny, Vct of Amiens46歲，母親Mathilde Mahaut De Crésecque22歲。他於1277年12月與Marguerite de Beaumetz在法國豪-德-福朗斯帕-德-卡拉斯波奧梅-利斯-洛格斯結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他去世於1304年10月15日在義大利武恩布利亞培盧吉亞培盧吉亞享年57歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jean Ier De Picquigny于1247年出生在法国豪-德-福朗斯索姆皮易尼时，他的父亲Gérard II de Picquigny, Vct of Amiens46岁，母亲Mathilde Mahaut De Crésecque22岁。他于1277年12月与Marguerite de Beaumetz在法国豪-德-福朗斯帕-德-卡拉斯波奧梅-利斯-洛格斯结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和5个女儿的父母。他去世于1304年10月15日在意大利武恩布利亞培盧吉亞培盧吉亞享年57岁。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Jean
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Like Jane and Joan , a medieval variant of Old French Je(h)anne. Towards the end of the Middle Ages this form became largely confined to Scotland. In the 20th century it became more widely used in the English-speaking world and enjoyed a period of great popularity, but it is now out of fashion. Among numerous well-known and influential bearers are the British novelists Jean Plaidy ( Eleanor Hibbert , 1910–93 ) and Jean Rhys ( Ella Gwendolen Rees Williams , 1894–1979 ), British actress Jean Simmons ( 1929–2010 ), and American-born actress Jean Seberg ( 1938–79 ). It is also found as a variant spelling of the masculine name Gene .

variants.name: John
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

nameMeanings.name: Ier
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: and Norwegian vernacular form of   Peter .

variants.name: Peter
meanings: form of the name of the best-known of all Christ's apostles, traditionally regarded as the founder of the Christian Church. The name derives, via Latin, from Greek petros ‘stone, rock’. This is used as a translation of the Aramaic byname Cephas, given to the apostle Simon son of Jona, to distinguish him from another of the same name ( Simon Zelotes ). ‘When Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone’ (John 1:42). According to Matthew 16:17–18, Christ says more explicitly, ‘Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona … thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church’. The name has been in continuous use since the Middle Ages.

en.lifeSummary: When Jean Ier De Picquigny was born in 1247, in Picquigny, Somme, Hauts-de-France, France, his father, Gérard II de Picquigny, Vct of Amiens, was 46 and his mother, Mathilde Mahaut De Crésecque, was 22. He married Marguerite de Beaumetz in December 1277, in Beaumetz-lès-Loges, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, France. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 5 daughters. He died on 15 October 1304, in Perugia, Perugia, Umbria, Italy, at the age of 57.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jean Ier De Picquigny est né en 1247, à Picquigny, Somme, Hauts-de-France, France, son père, Gérard II de Picquigny, Vct of Amiens, avait 46 ans et sa mère, Mathilde Mahaut De Crésecque, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé Marguerite de Beaumetz en décembre 1277, à Beaumetz-lès-Loges, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, France. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 5 filles. Il est décédé le 15 octobre 1304, à Perugia, Perugia, Umbria, Italie, à l’âge de 57 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jean Ier De Picquigny 1247 in Picquigny, Somme, Hauts-de-France, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Gérard II de Picquigny, Vct of Amiens 46 und seine Mutter Mathilde Mahaut De Crésecque 22. Er hat im Dezember 1277 in Beaumetz-lès-Loges, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, Frankreich Marguerite de Beaumetz geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Er starb am 15. Oktober 1304 im Alter von 57 Jahren in Perugia, Perugia, Umbria, Italien.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jean Ier De Picquigny è nato il nel 1247 nel Picquigny, Somme, Hauts-de-France, Francia, il suo padre, Gérard II de Picquigny, Vct of Amiens, aveva 46 anni e il suo madre, Mathilde Mahaut De Crésecque, aveva 22 anni. Lui sposato Marguerite de Beaumetz nel dicembre 1277, nel Beaumetz-lès-Loges, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 5 figlie. Morì il 15 ottobre 1304, nel Perugia, Perugia, Umbria, Italia, all'età di 57 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Jean Ier De Picquignyが1247年にフランスハウトス-デ-フランスソンメピククイグニーで生まれた時，父Gérard II de Picquigny, Vct of Amiensは46歳，母Mathilde Mahaut De Crésecqueは22歳であった。1277年12月にフランスハウトス-デ-フランスプアス-デ-カレイスビーウメトズ-ルeス-ロジェズでMarguerite de Beaumetzと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼は1304年10月15日にイタリアウンブリアペルジアペルジアで57歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jean Ier De Picquigny(이)가 1247년 프랑스 하츠-데-프랜스 솜 피c키그니에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Gérard II de Picquigny, Vct of Amiens(은)는 46세였고, 그의 어머니 Mathilde Mahaut De Crésecque(은)는 22세였다. 그는 1277년 12월 프랑스 하츠-데-프랜스 파스-데-캘레이 보멧즈-e스-로그스에서 Marguerite de Beaumetz(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1304년 10월 15일 이탈리아 브리아 페루지아 페루지아에서 57세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jean Ier De Picquigny nasceu em 1247, em Picquigny, Somme, Hauts-de-France, França, seu pai, Gérard II de Picquigny, Vct of Amiens, tinha 46 anos e sua mãe, Mathilde Mahaut De Crésecque, tinha 22 anos. Ele casou-se com Marguerite de Beaumetz em dezembro de 1277, em Beaumetz-lès-Loges, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 5 filhas. Ele faleceu em 15 de outubro de 1304, em Perugia, Perugia, Umbria, Itália, com 57 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jean Ier De Picquigny родился в 1247 г. в местности пиккуигни, сомм, хауц-де-фрэнс, Франция, его отцу по имени Gérard II de Picquigny, Vct of Amiens было 46 лет, а матери, которую звали Mathilde Mahaut De Crésecque, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Marguerite de Beaumetz декабрь 1277 г. в местности бомец-лeс-логесь, пас-де-калеи, хауц-де-фрэнс, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 5 дочерей. Он умер 15 октября 1304 г. в местности пиругя, пиругя, умбря, Италия в возрасте 57 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jean Ier De Picquigny nació en 1247, en Picquigny, Somme, Hauts-de-France, Francia, su papá, Gérard II de Picquigny, Vct of Amiens, tenía 46 años y su mamá, Mathilde Mahaut De Crésecque, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Marguerite de Beaumetz en diciembre de 1277, en Beaumetz-lès-Loges, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 15 de octubre de 1304, en Perugia, Perugia, Umbria, Italia, a la edad de 57 años.
